title: Strait Below Neutral

mood.label: Uneasy calm đźŚĄď¸Ź
mood.summary: The world held just below neutral again, a fresh missile strike on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz and a super typhoon aimed at Taiwan pulling down against real diplomatic lift in Ankara and a new Pacific defence pact.

style.genre: motorik / dub-techno â€” a sampled Rhodes-ish electric piano laying ambiguous D melodic minor chords over a steady, driving TR-909 four-on-the-floor pulse with deep tape-echo, a sampled vibraphone tracing a circling topline in the gaps, a plucked upright bass walking under a soft sine sub and a low bowed-string pad holding the unease; melodic minor's raised sixth and seventh lifting the minor third just enough to keep the calm honest and unsettled â€” forward motion under a heavy sky, a day that tilts a shade down but refuses to fall

lines: Two missiles woke the water off Oman,
a carrier's engine-room aglow at dawn.
The barrel ticks three points and holds its breath,
the chip floor sinks, the record slides â€” not death,
just the strait pulled narrow and the needle low,
a truce still trying to become a vow.
We're strait below neutral, we don't fall,
a heavy sky, a steady drum, that's all.
Ankara pledges, Suva shakes a hand â€”
forty-two, and the day still learns to stand.
Bavi turns her eyewall toward the coast,
a metre of rain where the island needs it most.
El Obeid in darkness, Caracas counts its dead,
and a trial in Ituri offers thread by thread.
So carry the ledger open, both hands full,
the losses loud, the quiet wins still pull.
